sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Serotonin syndrome is a potentially life-threatening condition caused by increased serotonergic activity in the central nervous system often due to the use or interaction of certain medications or recreational drugs. It presents as a triad of autonomic instability, neuromuscular abnormalities, and altered mental status.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Confusion, altered mental status
- Abdominal pain
- Agitation, restlessness, clonus (ankles), hyperreflexia, incoordination, ataxia, rigidity
- Tachycardia, hypotension/hypertension
- Tachypnoea
- Dilated pupils, ocular clonus
- Fever, flushing
- Seizure, coma, death

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Investigating why/how the overdose occurred is important to rule out self-harm
- **Hunter Criteria (most validated prehospital diagnostic tool):** Serotonin toxicity is diagnosed when a serotonergic drug has been taken AND any of the following are present:
  - Spontaneous clonus
  - Inducible clonus PLUS agitation or diaphoresis
  - Ocular clonus PLUS agitation or diaphoresis
  - Tremor PLUS hyperreflexia
  - Hypertonia PLUS temperature >38°C PLUS ocular or inducible clonus
- Clonus (rhythmic muscle contractions, especially at the ankle) is the most discriminating feature - more specific than diaphoresis, tremor, or tachycardia alone
- More severe toxicity with progressive hyperthermia, rigidity, and seizures is life-threatening; sedation and rapid active cooling are the key prehospital priorities
- Citalopram and escitalopram can prolong QT interval and increase risk of torsades de pointes
- Cyproheptadine (serotonin antagonist) is a hospital treatment only

sections.title: Management – Primary
sections.content:
- Primary Survey
- Offer continuous support and reassurance if applicable
- Position patient appropriately
- Administer oxygen if indicated
- Initiate active cooling if the patient is hyperthermic
- Record vital signs and assess for clonus, rigidity, agitation, and reduced conscious state
- Have a low threshold for calling the on-call doctor or Poisons Information

sections.title: Management – Advanced
sections.content:
- Consider advanced airway
- Provide assisted ventilation if required
- Gain IV access
- Consider fluids if indicated
- Consider midazolam in consultation with the on-call doctor for agitation, clonus, or seizures
- Apply cardiac monitoring and 12 lead ECG for QTc prolongation; be aware that tremor may cause artefact
- Escalate early for hyperthermia, rigidity, seizures, or rapid deterioration

sections.title: Additional Information
sections.content: - Always offer engagement with Mind Matters or EAP if applicable
